# Name storing from different sources

: Ameya Aglawe
Created time: October 9, 2024 2:45 PM
Status: Not started
Last edited: October 11, 2024 11:26 AM

# **What problem are we solving?**

Currently, we don’t store user names from different sources (PAN, Aadhar, Bank) in a structured, queryable format. This leads to inefficiencies during analysis and debugging, as names must be individually retrieved from writing query for each source through Amazon CloudWatch, resulting in increased latency.

---

# **How do we measure success?**

- Reduction in man-hours required to analyze name match scores across different sources for weekly data.
- Decreased time spent debugging name mismatch issues.
- Ensuring 99% accuracy in name match scores across all sources.

---

# **How are others solving this problem?**

---

# **What is the solution?**

## Requirements overview (optional)

### User stories / User flow

## Tech Requirements

- We will store the following fields in a queryable format
    - PAN name
    - Aadhar name (KYC_POD)
    - Bank name (Penny drop)
    - PAN-Aadhar name match score
    - PAN-Bank name match score

- We will store the following fields in a queryable format
